verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase canonically anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation, placing its kinase domain on the cytoplasmic face — not the extracellular face. However, multiple studies document cancer-cell ecto-display of SRC on the outer leaflet of tumor cells (ecto-kinase phenomenon), where it is accessible to extracellular antibodies and inhibitors. This cancer-restricted outer-leaflet pool qualifies as cell_state_induced surface biology under the oncogenic transformation bucket, making contextual the correct call rather than no.
